case_name: Fred Rocba Break-In — Protocol SIFT Memory-Only Baseline
summary: Memory-only analysis of Rocba-Memory.raw (Windows 10, captured 2020-11-16 02:32:38 UTC). No malware, code injection, or rootkit detected. Two hostile external IPs (81.30.144.115 and 213.202.233.104) conducted an active RDP brute-force attack on 2020-11-16 — 3 days after the reported Nov 13 incident. Six cloud synchronization services (OneDrive x2, Google Drive x2, iCloud x3, Slack, Teams) were actively connected at capture time. MRC.exe (PID 29440, D:\Tools\MRC.exe) is a benign ROCBA course exercise tool. Memory captured 3 days post-incident; Nov 13 unauthorized access activity requires disk forensics.
suspicious_processes: MRC.exe (PID 29440) — non-standard path D:\Tools\MRC.exe; ROCBA course tool; benign
timeline: 2020-11-16 02:34:45 UTC — Multiple ESTABLISHED RDP sessions from both hostile IPs
analysis_notes: Memory was captured 2020-11-16 — 3 days after the reported Nov 13 incident.
Nov 13 unauthorized access event cannot be analyzed from memory alone.
Disk forensics required: event logs, browser history, LNK files, prefetch, shimcache, MFT.
No code injection detected in any process.
No malicious services or persistence in Run keys.
RDP brute-force (Nov 16) is a separate event from the Nov 13 break-in.
